bd.title: Bangladesh Eyes Austerity Amid High Inflation in Upcoming National Budget FY2024-25
bd.summary: Bangladesh is preparing to unveil its national budget for the fiscal year 2024-25 on June 6, with a strong focus on controlling persistent inflation and bolstering revenue collection. Finance officials indicate potential cuts in non-essential spending and a continued push for austerity measures to stabilize the economy. The budget aims to balance economic growth targets with the immediate challenge of rising living costs for citizens.

bd.title: Bangladesh Bank Maintains Tight Monetary Stance to Curb Inflation and Stabilize Forex Market
bd.summary: The Bangladesh Bank is continuing its hawkish monetary policy to address stubbornly high inflation and a volatile foreign exchange market. Recent measures include further interest rate hikes and efforts to manage liquidity, aiming to cool down price increases and ease pressure on the Bangladeshi Taka. Analysts anticipate a cautious approach from the central bank as it navigates complex economic headwinds.

bd.title: Persistent Heatwave Exacerbates Power Crisis Across Bangladesh, Triggering Widespread Load Shedding
bd.summary: Bangladesh continues to grapple with severe power shortages, exacerbated by a prolonged heatwave that has driven up demand across the nation. Frequent load shedding is disrupting daily life and economic activities, particularly in rural areas and major cities outside Dhaka. The government is struggling to meet the energy deficit despite efforts to increase generation capacity.

international.title: International Outcry Grows Over Israeli Strikes in Rafah Following ICJ Rulings
international.summary: International condemnation has intensified following recent Israeli airstrikes in Rafah, Gaza, which caused numerous civilian casualties, particularly after an attack on a displacement camp. The incidents have prompted widespread global protests and renewed calls for a ceasefire, with many nations urging Israel to comply with the International Court of Justice's provisional measures regarding the protection of civilians. The ongoing military operation in Rafah is escalating humanitarian concerns in the besieged enclave.

international.title: Ukraine Receives Fresh Western Aid as Russian Forces Advance in Eastern Front
international.summary: Ukraine is bolstered by new pledges of military and financial aid from Western allies, crucial for its ongoing defense against Russian aggression. Despite the aid, Russian forces continue to make tactical gains in several sectors along the eastern front, intensifying pressure on Ukrainian positions. Kyiv is urging partners to expedite delivery of critical air defense systems and ammunition to counter the renewed Russian offensive.

international.title: India Prepares for Vote Count as Marathon General Election Nears Conclusion
international.summary: India is on the verge of concluding its six-week-long general election, with the final phase of voting scheduled for June 1 and the vote count set for June 4. Prime Minister Narendra Modi's BJP alliance is widely expected to secure another term, though the opposition INDIA bloc has mounted a spirited challenge. The outcome will shape India's domestic and foreign policies for the next five years, impacting regional dynamics and global trade.
